name: Trail of the Enchanted Moor (PR-CU 119)
name_original: La Atalaya – circular por sendero de la Mora Encantada (PR-CU 119)
description: Hike the La Atalaya – circular by the path of the Enchanted Mora (PR-CU 119), a moderate 5.9-mile route with 712 feet of gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Torrejoncillo Del Rey offers a diverse landscape experience, transitioning from the cereal plains of La Mancha to the foothills of the Alcarria and Serranía regions. The area is characterized by a succession of hills and small valleys, including the valley of the Hortizuela river, which features natural water sources. Geological formations like clay loams with gypsum crystals and marls add to the varied terrain. This blend provides a dynamic environment for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Torrejoncillo Del Rey The most popular hiking route is Trail of the Enchanted Moor (PR-CU 119), a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the historical Roman gypsum mines, known for their well-preserved galleries. Another top favourite among local hikers is Windmill and Tajo-Segura transfer — circular from Zafra de Záncara, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.0 km) path. This circular route offers views of windmills and the surrounding countryside near Zafra de Záncara. Local hikers also love the Hiking through the fields of Palomares del Campo, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail leading through agricultural fields, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Hiking around Torrejoncillo Del Rey is defined by rolling hills, fertile valleys, and expansive plains, with opportunities to discover historical Roman mine remnants. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy field walks to moderate trails with varied elevation. The routes in Torrejoncillo Del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Torrejoncillo Del Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Torrejoncillo Del Rey?
A: There are nearly 10 hiking trails in Torrejoncillo Del Rey, offering a range of experiences from easy walks through fields to moderate routes exploring historical sites and natural features.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Torrejoncillo Del Rey?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, transitioning from the cereal plains of La Mancha to the foothills of the Alcarria and Serranía. You'll encounter a succession of hills and small valleys, including the Hortizuela river valley, and unique geological formations with clay loams and gypsum crystals. Elevated points often provide panoramic views, especially at sunset.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Torrejoncillo Del Rey has several easy trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154591619/hiking-through-the-fields-of-palomares-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking through the fields of Palomares del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is a 3.2-mile route that takes about 1 hour and 21 minutes, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can explore the valley of the Hortizuela river with its natural water sources and geological formations. The area is also known for its historical Roman gypsum mines, such as those explored on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013712311/trail-of-the-enchanted-moor-pr-cu-119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Enchanted Moor (PR-CU 119)&lt;/a&gt;. You might also find interesting rock-cut necropolises, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6174510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock-Cut Necropolis with Carved Symbol&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5693607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock-cut Necropolis of Buenafuente del Sistal&lt;/a&gt;, and viewpoints offering scenic vistas, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Verdelpino de Huete&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1146919415/windmill-and-tajo-segura-transfer-circular-from-zafra-de-zancara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmill and Tajo-Segura transfer — circular from Zafra de Záncara&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.0-mile path offering views of windmills and the surrounding countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Torrejoncillo Del Rey?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be hot, especially in the open plains, while winters are generally mild but can be chilly.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in rural Spain, including those around Torrejoncillo Del Rey, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected natural areas, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Torrejoncillo Del Rey?
A: The trails in Torrejoncillo Del Rey are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the historical Roman mine remnants, and the tranquil rural atmosphere.

Q: Are there any routes that explore historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013712311/trail-of-the-enchanted-moor-pr-cu-119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Enchanted Moor (PR-CU 119)&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading you through well-preserved historical Roman gypsum mines, known as 'Lapis Specularis'.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the transition from plains to hills offers many scenic spots. Elevated points, particularly near the Ermita or Cueva de la Esperanza, are highlighted for their panoramic views, especially beautiful at sunset. The area near Zafra de Záncara also offers routes with spectacular views.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Torrejoncillo Del Rey?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route and your pace. Shorter, easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1154591619/hiking-through-the-fields-of-palomares-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking through the fields of Palomares del Campo&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes, while moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013712311/trail-of-the-enchanted-moor-pr-cu-119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Enchanted Moor (PR-CU 119)&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 2 hours 43 minutes.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads, or is parking generally accessible?
A: While specific public transport options to individual trailheads might be limited in this rural area, parking is generally accessible in and around Torrejoncillo Del Rey and nearby villages, making it convenient for those traveling by car to access the hiking routes.
tips: Very close to the town of Torrejoncillo del Rey is the hill of La Atalaya with its peak above 1000 meters above sea level. The panoramic views are very beautiful in all directions, you can clearly appreciate the silhouette of the San Bartolomé hill and the valleys of the Cigüela and Valdepalomar rivers that come together very close to here in the town of Horcajada de la Torre.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the La Atalaya – circular by the path of the Enchanted Mora (PR-CU 119) trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. It involves a total elevation gain of approximately 217 meters over its 9.5-kilometer length, making it suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: Hikers generally complete the La Atalaya – circular by the path of the Enchanted Mora (PR-CU 119) in about 2 hours and 43 minutes, though this can vary based on individual pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is the La Atalaya – circular by the path of the Enchanted Mora (PR-CU 119) a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as indicated by its name, this is a circular route, meaning you will end your hike at the same point where you started.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a key highlight on this route is the View from La Atalaya, offering scenic vistas.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route largely follows the official trail known as "Sendero de la Mora Encantada," covering approximately 99% of its length.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the La Atalaya – circular by the path of the Enchanted Mora (PR-CU 119)?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal details for this trail are not provided, moderate trails in regions like Cuenca, Spain, are generally enjoyable during spring and autumn for pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder conditions.

faqs.answer: Specific information regarding dog-friendliness for this particular trail (PR-CU 119) is not available. It's always recommended to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding pets.

faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required for the La Atalaya – circular by the path of the Enchanted Mora (PR-CU 119) trail. Most public hiking trails in Spain do not require permits.

faqs.answer: Specific parking details or public transport options for the La Atalaya – circular by the path of the Enchanted Mora (PR-CU 119) are not provided. It is advisable to consult local maps or resources for trailhead access points and parking availability in the Cuenca region.

faqs.answer: While detailed terrain descriptions for this specific trail are not available, as a moderate hike with a 217-meter elevation gain, you can expect varied natural paths, potentially including some inclines and descents typical of a circular route in a natural area.
